card.intuition: A fixed learning rate is rarely optimal throughout training. Start large to make fast progress; decay over time for fine-grained convergence. Common schedules: step decay (halve every k epochs), exponential decay, cosine annealing (oscillate to escape local minima), and warmup (start small, ramp up, then decay).
card.analogy: Adjusting a drill bit: use a large bit first to clear the bulk of material, then switch to a finer bit for precision work. Learning rate schedules apply the same principle — broad strokes early, fine adjustments late.
card.time_complexity: O(1) to compute lr at any step
when_to_use: Cosine annealing with warm restarts for finding multiple good minima
Linear warmup + decay for transformer training
Step decay for CNNs when validation loss plateaus
gotchas: Warmup is essential for large-batch training and transformers — starting with a large lr can destabilise early training
Cyclical learning rates can sometimes find better minima than monotone decay
Learning rate is the most important hyperparameter — schedule shape matters less than the range

exercise.prompt: Implement three learning rate schedules: step decay, cosine annealing, and linear warmup+decay.
exercise.solution:
lr0 * 0.5 * (1 + math.cos(math.pi * step / T_max))
(total_steps - step) / (total_steps - warmup_steps)
hints: Cosine annealing: lr = lr0 * 0.5 * (1 + cos(π * step / T_max)) — starts at lr0, ends at 0
After warmup, linearly decay from lr_max to 0: multiply by (total_steps - step) / (total_steps - warmup_steps)
exercise.test_code:
import math
# Step decay
assert step_decay(0.1, 0) == 0.1
assert step_decay(0.1, 10) == 0.05
assert step_decay(0.1, 20) == 0.025

# Cosine annealing
assert math.isclose(cosine_annealing(0.1, 0, 100), 0.1, rel_tol=1e-6)
assert math.isclose(cosine_annealing(0.1, 100, 100), 0.0, abs_tol=1e-9)
assert cosine_annealing(0.1, 50, 100) < 0.1  # decreasing

# Warmup + decay
assert warmup_then_decay(0.1, 0, 10, 100) == 0.0
assert math.isclose(warmup_then_decay(0.1, 10, 10, 100), 0.1)
assert warmup_then_decay(0.1, 100, 10, 100) == 0.0
print('PASS')
